ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: “Buŋkobiri maa duubu jemani, m daa zaasini zaasim, a nye maa, gbeesi dima la daa n bani chericheri, ka seesi gba mari booma.
translated: The world is full of lies, I have witnessed it myself; even the stars in the sky are not permanent, and the moon also has its dark spots.

text: Adam yuma kobsiwai ni pisita nyaaŋa n na o kpi.
translated: Adam's mother died after giving birth to him.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: A nyaaŋa, o dogi bipuŋŋu, ka digili o yuuri Dayina.
translated: Afterwards, he took the flag, and raised the Chinese flag.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Yisaaka mari Rabeeka kpe o ma gbana sugiri puwa, a naag u loobi o poga, ka daa n kpabira u ga. O ma kum sufusaaŋu n daa mari u, sufupeelim pa ta.
translated: Isaac loved Rebecca very much, but she was barren, and he prayed to God for her. God heard his prayer, and Rebecca became pregnant.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Jemanso zaa ka sagbana ti pili, sasuwa maa ni yi,
translated: Every time the sky darkens, the rain will fall.
